meta-distractor.value: Skip the bear case because it weakens the rating
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Skip the bear case because it weakens the rating', 'Omitting the bear case removes the most useful part of the sensitivity analysis. Compliance and any thoughtful client expects to see what would invalidate the call.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Variant perception risk
meta-distractor.value: Ignore the print because variant perception always takes time to be vindicated
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore the print because variant perception always takes time to be vindicated', '"Always" is the tell. Some variant perceptions take time; others were wrong. The discipline is to know which one you have, which requires going back to the sourcing.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Earnings call question craft
meta-distractor.value: Skip the question and email IR after the call for the answer privately
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Skip the question and email IR after the call for the answer privately', 'Asking publicly is the value of being on the call — both for the answer itself and for the public signal you create about what you are watching. Off-line follow-up also risks select...

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: My freedom and yours
meta-distractor.value: Ignore others, since each person’s freedom is totally separate
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Ignore others, since each person’s freedom is totally separate', 'Beauvoir stresses that freedoms are interdependent, not isolated.', 'Freedoms are linked, not sealed off from one another.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Conservatism
meta-distractor.value: Ignore potential losses until they are legally certain.
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Ignore potential losses until they are legally certain.', 'Conservatism recognizes probable losses early, even before legal certainty.', 'Anticipate likely losses promptly.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Normalize a stock comp swing
meta-distractor.value: Ignore stock comp entirely because it is non-cash
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Ignore stock comp entirely because it is non-cash', 'Non-cash does not mean costless: stock comp dilutes shareholders and is a real economic expense most analysts keep in operating costs. Excluding it overstates margins.', 'Non-cash is not free. Many ...

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Deferred revenue timing
meta-distractor.chapter: Revenue, Expense, and Working Capital Forecasts
meta-distractor.value: Ignore it until the cash is spent
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Ignore it until the cash is spent', 'Unearned cash creates an obligation to deliver service; ignoring it omits a real liability and the future revenue recognition.', 'Collecting cash for undelivered service always creates a liability to track.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Conditional Proof
meta-distractor.value: Assume the consequent and derive the antecedent
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Assume the consequent and derive the antecedent', 'That would prove the converse, not the original conditional.', 'Start with what appears before the arrow.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Conditional Proof
meta-distractor.value: Assume the conclusion is false in every proof
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Assume the conclusion is false in every proof', 'That is closer to reductio or tableaux strategy.', 'Conditional proof uses a temporary antecedent assumption.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Systematic debugging of a failed build
meta-distractor.value: Assume it is unfixable and start a new project
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Assume it is unfixable and start a new project', 'Giving up forfeits the chance to find a simple cause like a loose wire.', 'Diagnose systematically before abandoning the build.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Risk assessment before building
meta-distractor.value: Skip safety checks to save time
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Skip safety checks to save time', 'Skipping checks is exactly how accidents happen.', 'Risk assessment comes before, not after, a launch.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Value of peer critique
meta-distractor.value: Ignore it because it is your project, not theirs
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: miss('Ignore it because it is your project, not theirs', 'Dismissing feedback wastes a chance to improve the design.', 'Peer critique is meant to strengthen the work.'),

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Channel checks scope and limits
meta-distractor.value: Ignore the three positive readings because 17 locations had inventory
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore the three positive readings because 17 locations had inventory', 'The 17 locations with inventory are also data — but ignoring the three flags treats every observation symmetrically when the question is whether tail signals are pointing at a real t...

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Separating catalyst from steady-state DCF
meta-distractor.value: Ignore the catalyst because DCFs should reflect steady-state assumptions only
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore the catalyst because DCFs should reflect steady-state assumptions only', 'Ignoring a defined catalyst event throws away the structure of the trade. A catalyst with a known date and known payoff is exactly what HFs are paid to model.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Sensitivity bands around the implied case
meta-distractor.value: Pick the case closest to the implied numbers and call it the base case
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Pick the case closest to the implied numbers and call it the base case', 'Anchoring the base case on the implied numbers is circular — it just reproduces the current price as "fair". The discipline is to assess the implied claim independently.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Momentum factor contribution in a reversal month
meta-distractor.value: Ignore the attribution because factor models do not capture discretionary investing
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore the attribution because factor models do not capture discretionary investing', 'Discretionary books still carry factor exposure whether the PM measures it or not — that is precisely why the attribution exists. Dismissing the table is how a hidden f...

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: When the residual flips negative
meta-distractor.value: Ignore it — alpha is mean-reverting and one year of negative residual is noise
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore it — alpha is mean-reverting and one year of negative residual is noise', 'One year is statistically thin but it is the most recent data point, and dismissing it as noise is exactly how funds miss a deteriorating process until two years too late. I...

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Reading a crowding score
meta-distractor.value: Ignore the score because crowding is not a fundamental factor
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore the score because crowding is not a fundamental factor', 'Path matters in any position with a finite hold horizon. Ignoring crowding because it is "non-fundamental" leaves the PM exposed to a class of drawdown the rest of the framework was supposed...

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Grading each position monthly
meta-distractor.value: Skip the grading exercise — discretionary PMs know their book and grades add bureaucracy
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Skip the grading exercise — discretionary PMs know their book and grades add bureaucracy', 'The exercise forces discipline. Without it, PMs are systematically slow to cut thesis-failed names and quick to cut thesis-on-track losers. The grade is the struct...

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Proof by contradiction for sqrt(2)
meta-distractor.value: Assume sqrt(2) = 2, then 2 = 4, which is impossible.
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Assume sqrt(2) = 2, then 2 = 4, which is impossible.', 'sqrt(2) is not 2 to begin with. This sets up an irrelevant contradiction, not the standard rationality argument.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Conflicting sources
meta-distractor.value: Assume both sources are useless because they disagree.
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Assume both sources are useless because they disagree.', 'Disagreement is informative, not a reason to discard both. Historians weigh evidence to reach calibrated conclusions.']],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Creative algebra lab
meta-distractor.value: Ignore the result and start a different unit
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore the result and start a different unit', 'The point is to understand what went wrong here.', 'Use the impossible answer as a clue to debug the model.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Opening principle
meta-distractor.value: Ignore king safety until the endgame
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore king safety until the endgame', 'King safety matters early.', 'Castling and central control are core basics.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Difficulty tuning
meta-distractor.value: Ignore all pacing and come out of nowhere
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore all pacing and come out of nowhere', 'Sharp surprises can work, but pure randomness often feels bad.', 'Difficulty should feel legible.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Good process
meta-distractor.value: Ignore clues that seem inconvenient
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore clues that seem inconvenient', 'Inconvenient clues are often the useful ones.', 'Every clue should update the grid somehow.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Share before checking
meta-distractor.value: Assume all rumors are true
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Assume all rumors are true', 'Rumors need verification.', 'Use reliable sources.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Changing your mind
meta-distractor.value: Ignore the schedule because changing your mind is illegal
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Ignore the schedule because changing your mind is illegal', 'Changing beliefs with evidence is rational.', 'Better evidence should matter.'],

meta-distractor.label: Meta distractor
meta-distractor.title: Best argument habit
meta-distractor.value: Assume your first thought is perfect
meta-distractor.detail: Wrong answer is about answering strategy rather than content.
meta-distractor.snippet: ['Assume your first thought is perfect', 'First thoughts can be wrong.', 'Revise and support claims.'],
